description: Toast notification surface built on the sonner library. The Toaster renders an ordered list of toasts inside a region labelled "Notifications". Each toast is role="status" (or role="alert" for error-level), so they're announced via polite / assertive live regions without stealing focus. Theme is mirrored from next-themes.

keyboard.action: Moves focus to the toast region (sonner default, user-dismissible focus target).
When focus is in the toast region, moves to the toast's action / cancel buttons.

focus.notes: Toasts do not steal focus. When the user presses F6, focus moves into the toast region so keyboard users can reach action / dismiss buttons.

announcements: Each toast is announced via role="status" (polite) by default, or role="alert" (assertive) for error toasts.
The action button and cancel button carry their own labels.
screenReader.notes: Sonner's default announcements are concise. For long messages, provide a description that keeps the primary message short, since live regions can be interrupted if the user acts quickly.

contrast.text: Uses group-[.toaster]:bg-background / text-foreground; description uses text-muted-foreground. All meet WCAG AA
contrast.ui: Border uses border-border; action button uses primary tokens. Both meet AA non-text contrast

motion.reducedMotion: Sonner animates enter / exit with slide + fade. The library respects prefers-reduced-motion natively, collapsing animations to a plain fade when the user opts out.

consumerResponsibilities: Use toast.error for destructive / error states so the assertive live region is used.
Keep messages short; toasts auto-dismiss, so long text gets cut off before AT finishes reading.
For actions that require a decision (not a confirmation), use a Dialog / AlertDialog instead; toasts can be missed.
Mount <Toaster /> once at the app root; additional Toasters will double-render toasts.
